body.title: CLOSED: NE grader: low-scoring cycles write TWM diagnostic entry naming failing dimension
body.result: NE_QUALITY_ALERT pushed to TWM at salience=0.75 category=ne_quality when overall<0.4 or any dim<0.3
body.description:
The NE grader (_grade_ne_output in coa.py) scores three dimensions and escalates when overall < 0.5, but produces no structured diagnostic for the NE to act on. The grader result should feed back into cognition so Igor can self-correct.

In coa.py, after _grade_ne_output() returns, when overall < 0.4 or any dimension < 0.3, call cortex.twm_push() with content_csb='NE_QUALITY_ALERT|dim=[failing_dim]|score=[score]' at salience=0.75 category='ne_quality'. The NE sees this on the next cycle and can adapt its approach. Existing channel escalation unchanged.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/coa.py (MEDIUM inertia)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/coding (MEDIUM inertia); theigors/rules/database (twm_push via cortex, Postgres)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: twm_push() call after low grader score in _ne_worker. Out of scope: NE response strategy to quality alerts (emergent from NE seeing TWM content).
**Test plan:** Mock _grade_ne_output() returning {'memory_retrieval_quality': 0.2, 'context_assembly_quality': 0.6, 'output_coherence': 0.7, 'overall_score': 0.5} — cortex.twm_push() called with content containing 'NE_QUALITY_ALERT|dim=memory_retrieval_quality'. Channel escalation still fires. Dashboard TWM panel shows the alert entry.
